goal: Implement, internally wire, and test the real structured-family write path for controlled targets (test-owned fixtures, copied config trees, temp configs) with backup, restore, rollback, and reread verification proof, while keeping the active real Hyprland config unwritable.
userDecision: Approve controlled-target real-write implementation, actual executor wiring scaffold, and internal executor wiring; active real config writes remain unapproved.
productionReadinessDecision: not production ready for active real config writes
targetPolicies: TestOwnedFixtureTarget: writable in the controlled phase when the controlled root is a tests/fixtures-style or temp path
CopiedConfigTreeTarget: writable in the controlled phase when the controlled root is under the system temp directory
TemporaryConfigTarget: writable in the controlled phase when the controlled root is under the system temp directory
ActiveRealConfigTarget: always rejected; any path that resolves under the active Hyprland config directory is reclassified to this kind and refused
UnknownTarget: always rejected; path escapes, symlink escapes, targets outside the controlled root, disallowed roots, and inconsistent declared kinds resolve to this kind
policy field activeRealConfigWritable is a constant false, not a switch
declared kinds are verified against the path; a declared temporary target pointing at the active config is reclassified and rejected
writeExecutorBehavior: consumes an accepted, unblocked, plan-ready staged apply plan; blocked, rejected, invalid, already-executed, or mutation-reporting plans are refused as unsafe
verifies approval first; the approval model cannot approve active real config writes (that flag being true is itself an error)
classifies the target and refuses any non-writable policy before reading a single byte
requires backup, restore, and verification plans; any missing or weakened plan fails closed
requires the backup path to stay inside the controlled root
creates a byte-exact verified backup before writing
replaces only the family's records; comments, unknown syntax, other families, and scalar lines are preserved
rereads the written target through the parser/projection path and verifies the intended rendered records are present
restores the original bytes automatically when post-write verification fails
restore execution rereads and verifies byte-exact restoration plus parser/projection record restoration
emits a write receipt with backup, verification, and rollback proof and an audit record
never calls hyprctl, never reloads Hyprland, never runs commands, never mutates runtime
receipt flags activeRealConfigTouched, hyprctlReloadRun, and runtimeMutated are constant false
backupRestoreProof: tests prove a backup file is created before the write and preserves the original bytes exactly
tests prove restore returns the controlled target to its original bytes
tests prove backup paths outside the controlled root are refused before any write
round-trip proof runs for all seven structured families against copied temp targets
rollbackRecoveryProof: restore_structured_family_controlled_write executes rollback for controlled targets and verifies it
the round-trip executor carries rollback proof inside the receipt
post-write verification failure triggers automatic restoration of the original bytes
rollback proof exists only for controlled targets; no active-config rollback path exists
rereadVerificationProof: post-write reread goes through parse_hyprland_config_text, CurrentConfigSnapshot::from_parsed, and structured_family_projection
post-write verification compares reread family records to the intended rendered records
post-restore reread verifies the original family records and byte-exact content
non-family lines (comments, unknown syntax) are proven preserved across the write
failureModeProof: approving the active real config is itself rejected
unsafe (blocked/rejected) staged apply plans are refused at plan build time
tampered staged-apply linkage is refused at execution time
active real config targets are refused
targets outside the controlled root are refused
post-write verification failure restores the original bytes and reports the failure
sourceGuards: controlled-write sources contain no hyprctl invocation, no reload path, no command runner, no process spawning
controlled-write sources contain no GTK widgets, buttons, or click handlers
controlled-write sources contain no active real config path literal
controlled-write sources do not call write_flow, apply_setting_change, or the inert safe-write scaffold
the controlled executor is unreachable from src/main.rs, src/write_flow.rs, and all UI sources
no live UI Apply control exists for structured-family writes
main, v0.1.0, and dist/v0.1.0 remain unmodified
nextRecommendedWork: Stop for explicit user decision: approve or reject a first active real config write pilot for structured families.
